referencedTypes.name: SelectContentProps
referencedTypes.resolvedType: { position?: SelectPosition; bodyLock?: boolean; popupProps?: SelectPopupProps; placement?: Placement; side?: Side; sideOffset?: number; sideFlip?: boolean; align?: Align; alignOffset?: number; alignFlip?: boolean; avoidCollisions?: boolean; collisionBoundary?: Element | (Element | null)[] | null; collisionPadding?: Padding; arrowPadding?: number; hideShiftedArrow?: boolean; sticky?: 'partial' | 'always'; hideWhenDetached?: boolean; positionStrategy?: 'fixed' | 'absolute'; updatePositionStrategy?: 'always' | 'optimized'; disableUpdateOnLayoutShift?: boolean; prioritizePosition?: boolean; reference?: ReferenceElement; disableOutsidePointerEvents?: boolean; forceMount?: boolean }

members.name: position
members.description:
The positioning mode to use `item-aligned (default)` - behaves similarly to a native MacOS menu by positioning content relative to the active item.
`popper` - positions content in the same way as our other primitives, for example `Popover` or `DropdownMenu`.

members.name: bodyLock
members.description: The document.body will be lock, and scrolling will be disabled.

members.name: popupProps
members.description: Properties forwarded to the popup element.

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.

members.name: side
members.description:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.

members.name: sideOffset
members.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

members.name: sideFlip
members.description: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.

members.name: align
members.description: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.

members.name: alignOffset
members.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

members.name: alignFlip
members.description:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.

members.name: avoidCollisions
members.description: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

members.name: collisionBoundary
members.description:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.

members.name: collisionPadding
members.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

members.name: arrowPadding
members.description:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.

members.name: hideShiftedArrow
members.description:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.

members.name: sticky
members.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

members.name: hideWhenDetached
members.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

members.name: positionStrategy
members.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

members.name: updatePositionStrategy
members.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.name: disableUpdateOnLayoutShift
members.description: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.

members.name: prioritizePosition
members.description: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.

members.name: reference
members.description:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.

members.name: disableOutsidePointerEvents
members.description:
When `true`, hover/focus/click interactions will be disabled on elements outside the `DismissableLayer`. Users will
need to click twice on outside elements to interact with them: once to close the `DismissableLayer`, and again to
trigger the element.

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

members.description: Placement.

members.description: Properties forwarded to the popup element.

members.description:
Will add `nonce` attribute to the style tag which can be used by Content Security Policy.
If omitted, inherits globally from `ConfigProvider`.

members.name: value
members.description: The value given as data when submitted with a `name`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with the item.

members.name: textValue
members.description:
Optional text used for typeahead purposes. By default the typeahead behavior will use the `.textContent` of the
`SelectItemText` part. Use this when the content is complex, or you have non-textual content inside.

members.description: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

members.name: separatorProps
members.description: Properties forwarded to the separator element.

members.name: arrowProps
members.description: Properties forwarded to the arrow element.

members.name: open
members.description: The controlled open state of the Select. Can be bind as `v-model:open`.

members.name: defaultOpen
members.description: The open state of the select when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.

members.name: dir
members.description:
The reading direction of the combobox when applicable.
If omitted, inherits globally from `ConfigProvider` or assumes LTR (left-to-right) reading mode.

members.name: autocomplete
members.description: Native html input `autocomplete` attribute.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with Select

members.name: modelValue
members.description:
The controlled value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to control the state of the items. Can be bound with `v-model`

members.name: defaultValue
members.description:
The default value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to set the initial state of the items.

members.name: multiple
members.description:
Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

This prop will overwrite the inferred type from `modelValue` and `defaultValue`.

members.name: clearable
members.description: Whether selected item can be cleared when `multiple` is false.

members.name: selectionBehavior
members.description: How multiple selection should behave in the collection.

members.name: name
members.description: The name of the field. Submitted with its owning form as part of a name/value pair.

members.name: required
members.description: When `true`, indicates that the user must set the value before the owning form can be submitted.

referencedTypes.name: Event
referencedTypes.description:
The **`Event`** interface represents an event which takes place on an EventTarget.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Event)

callables.name: escapeKeyDown
callables.description: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: KeyboardEvent
referencedTypes.description:
**`KeyboardEvent`** objects describe a user interaction with the keyboard; each event describes a single interaction between the user and a key (or combination of a key with modifier keys) on the keyboard. The event type (keydown, keypress, or keyup) identifies what kind of keyboard activity occurred.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent)

callables.name: pointerDownOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when the a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: select
callables.description:
Event handler called when the selecting item.
It can be prevented by calling `event.preventDefault`.

referencedTypes.name: SelectItemEvent
referencedTypes.type: CustomEvent<{ originalEvent: PointerEvent | KeyboardEvent; value: T; }>

**`KeyboardEvent`** objects describe a user interaction with the keyboard; each event describes a single interaction between the user and a key (or combination of a key with modifier keys) on the keyboard. [MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent)

props.name: SelectContentProps
props.resolvedType: { position?: SelectPosition; bodyLock?: boolean; popupProps?: SelectPopupProps; placement?: Placement; side?: Side; sideOffset?: number; sideFlip?: boolean; align?: Align; alignOffset?: number; alignFlip?: boolean; avoidCollisions?: boolean; collisionBoundary?: Element | (Element | null)[] | null; collisionPadding?: Padding; arrowPadding?: number; hideShiftedArrow?: boolean; sticky?: 'partial' | 'always'; hideWhenDetached?: boolean; positionStrategy?: 'fixed' | 'absolute'; updatePositionStrategy?: 'always' | 'optimized'; disableUpdateOnLayoutShift?: boolean; prioritizePosition?: boolean; reference?: ReferenceElement; disableOutsidePointerEvents?: boolean; forceMount?: boolean }

members.name: position
members.description:
The positioning mode to use `item-aligned (default)` - behaves similarly to a native MacOS menu by positioning content relative to the active item.
`popper` - positions content in the same way as our other primitives, for example `Popover` or `DropdownMenu`.

members.name: bodyLock
members.description: The document.body will be lock, and scrolling will be disabled.

members.name: popupProps
members.description: Properties forwarded to the popup element.

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.

members.name: side
members.description:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.

members.name: sideOffset
members.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

members.name: sideFlip
members.description: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.

members.name: align
members.description: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.

members.name: alignOffset
members.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

members.name: alignFlip
members.description:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.

members.name: avoidCollisions
members.description: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

members.name: collisionBoundary
members.description:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.

referencedTypes.name: Element
referencedTypes.description:
**`Element`** is the most general base class from which all element objects (i.e., objects that represent elements) in a Document inherit. It only has methods and properties common to all kinds of elements. More specific classes inherit from Element.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Element)

members.name: collisionPadding
members.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

members.name: arrowPadding
members.description:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.

members.name: hideShiftedArrow
members.description:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.

members.name: sticky
members.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

members.name: hideWhenDetached
members.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

members.name: positionStrategy
members.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

members.name: updatePositionStrategy
members.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.name: disableUpdateOnLayoutShift
members.description: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.

members.name: prioritizePosition
members.description: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.

members.name: reference
members.description:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.

members.name: disableOutsidePointerEvents
members.description:
When `true`, hover/focus/click interactions will be disabled on elements outside the `DismissableLayer`. Users will
need to click twice on outside elements to interact with them: once to close the `DismissableLayer`, and again to
trigger the element.

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

members.name: value
members.description: The value given as data when submitted with a `name`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with the item.

members.name: textValue
members.description:
Optional text used for typeahead purposes. By default the typeahead behavior will use the `.textContent` of the
`SelectItemText` part. Use this when the content is complex, or you have non-textual content inside.

members.name: asChild
members.description: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

members.name: as
members.description: The element or component this component should render as. Can be overwrite by `asChild`

referencedTypes.description:
A type used in public APIs where a component type is expected.
The constructor type is an artificial type returned by defineComponent().

props.name: SelectRootProps
props.resolvedType: { open?: boolean; defaultOpen?: boolean; dir?: Direction; autocomplete?: string; disabled?: boolean; modelValue?: (M extends true ? T[] : T); defaultValue?: (M extends true ? T[] : T); multiple?: M; clearable?: boolean; selectionBehavior?: SelectionBehavior; name?: string; required?: boolean }

members.name: open
members.description: The controlled open state of the Select. Can be bind as `v-model:open`.

members.name: defaultOpen
members.description: The open state of the select when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.

members.name: dir
members.description:
The reading direction of the combobox when applicable.
If omitted, inherits globally from `ConfigProvider` or assumes LTR (left-to-right) reading mode.

members.name: autocomplete
members.description: Native html input `autocomplete` attribute.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with Select

members.name: modelValue
members.description:
The controlled value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to control the state of the items. Can be bound with `v-model`

members.name: defaultValue
members.description:
The default value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to set the initial state of the items.

members.name: multiple
members.description:
Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

This prop will overwrite the inferred type from `modelValue` and `defaultValue`.

members.name: clearable
members.description: Whether selected item can be cleared when `multiple` is false.

members.name: selectionBehavior
members.description: How multiple selection should behave in the collection.

members.name: name
members.description: The name of the field. Submitted with its owning form as part of a name/value pair.

members.name: required
members.description: When `true`, indicates that the user must set the value before the owning form can be submitted.
